heading: LIMITATION OF SCHOOL TAX ON HOMESTEADS OF ELDERLY OR DISABLED
operative_text: The tax officials shall appraise the property to which this section applies and calculate taxes as on other property, but if the tax so calculated exceeds the limitation imposed by this section, the tax imposed is the amount of the tax as limited by this section, except as otherwise provided by this section. A school district may not increase the total annual amount of ad valorem tax it imposes on the residence homestead of an individual 65 years of age or older or on the residence homestead of an individual who is disabled, as defined by Section 11.13, above the amount of the tax it imposed in the first tax year in which the individual qualified that residence homestead for the applicable exemption provided by Section 11.13(c) for an individual who is 65 years of age or older or is disabled.

defined_terms.definition_excerpt: the maximum compressed rate of a school district as calculated under Section 48.2551, Education Code.

_note: Full statute text provided; all subsections (a) through (p) are complete. No truncation detected.
